titleStmt.title: SII 19.240: original edition by G.V. Srinivasa Rao, Dines Chandra Sircar and G.S. Gai
No. 240. (A.R. No. 170 of 1904.) TIRUVAKKARAI, VILLUPURAM TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT. AT THE NORTHERN ENTRANCE OF THE FIRST prākāra OF THE CHANDRAMAUḶĪŚVARA TEMPLE.

summary.p: This inscription records the decision of the peruṅguṟi-perumakkaḷ (assembly) of Tribhuvanamahādēvi-chaturvēdimaṅgalam, granting 4 vēli of land with a big well in it, a dry field and also some other land irrigated by the tank at Tiruvakkarai, for the expenses of worship and offerings to the gods Kaṟṟaḷi-Paramasvāmi and Vishṇu-Bhaṭṭāraka in the temple of that village; and also the appointment of 4 persons for performing worship therein and four others for music during the śrībali service, with provision made for their maintenance with the land gift got at the village Āḻiyūr. The main village was evidently called after Tribhuvanamahādēvi the queen of Parāntaka I. This may be a record of the reign of Uttama-Chōḷa.

revisionDesc.change: Further conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)
Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti śrī .
kopparakesaripan
markki yāṇṭu pa
ttāvatu śrī tri
bhuvaṉamahāde
vicaturvetimaṅ
kalattu tiruva
kkarai kaṟṟaḷipara
masvāmikaḷum
viṣṇupaṭṭālaka
rum Āḻiyūr
kkāka nilavaḻi
parivarttaṉai
yāl peṟṟu
ṭaiya Iraṇṭu
aṣṭakattum
yiraṇṭu śrīko
yilum A
rccikka Uṭai
ya kulaṅkiḻārka
ḷa kaṇṭi nilakaṇ
ṭiyum nākaṉ kaṟṟaḷi
yum kumārasū(svā)mi peru
māṉum kaṟṟaḷi cettu
paṭṭaṉum
ceyyakkaṭavā
rāka Arcc(ā)ṉā
bhoga paṅku
nālum śrī
pali koṭṭum U
vacca paṅku nālu
m cantri(ntrā)titta
vaṟ niṟkka kuṟu
mpuranti peru
māṉ kaṇkā
ṇiyoṭum ci
lālekai ce
yvittu kuṭu
ttom pe
ruṅkuṟipperu
makkaḷḷom
tiruvakkaraiyi
l Ūrakanilattil
Idevarkaḷu
kku Iruvarkku
teṉkaḻaniyi
l nilam nālu
veliyum pe
ruṅkeṇiyum
Amukkuṭṭat
til kollai
nilamum tiruvi
nāḷ Erikiḻ nilamu
m .
Digital edition of SII 19.240 by converted to DHARMA conventions by Emmanuel Francis.
